channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has dug in on its contention that his government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite U.S. Supreme Court and lower court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge orders federal agencies to release billions of dollars from two Biden-era initiatives
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to release billions of dollars meant to finance climate and infrastructure projects across the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department can cut funding for legal guidance for people facing deportation, US judge says
item.description: A federal judge has allowed the U.S. Department of Justice to temporarily stop funding legal education programs for people facing deportation or immigration court while a lawsuit brought by the organizations that provide the service moves forward in court.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone And Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: White House slams Ivy League institutions for 'egregious illegal behavior' amid Trump feud with Harvard
item.description: The Trump administration is going head-to-head with Harvard University to install changes to the academic institution’s governance and admissions process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House criticizes 'rogue' judge upholding parole program 'completely abused' by migrants
item.description: Karoline Leavitt expressed the administration's frustration with "rogue" judges after it was blocked from revoking the legal status and work permits of more than 530,000 migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyers for Harvard in Trump administration dispute are no strangers to high-profile legal matters
item.description: The two attorneys representing Harvard University in a pitched fight with the Trump administration are no strangers to the spotlight or to Washington investigations that reach into the White House.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat city council sues mayor for allowing ICE into major American prison
item.description: The Democrat-controlled city council of New York City is suing Mayor Eric Adams for cooperating with the Trump administration by allowing ICE to conduct investigations at Rikers Island Prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC claims Republicans will 'trick' Americans into identity politics, stoking racial divisions
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez told rally goers Monday evening not to let Republicans "trick" them into thinking they are defined by their race or other immutable characteristics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Council sues to block Mayor Eric Adams from allowing ICE office on Rikers Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City lawmakers are suing to block Mayor Eric Adams from allowing federal immigration agents to operate within the Rikers Island jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: “Threatening to harm public officials is a criminal act that cannot be excused as political discourse,” acting U.S. Attorney Richard Moultrie, Jr. said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders, AOC set for Bakersfield stop of 'Fighting Oligarchy' tour
item.description: Sanders and Ocasio-Cortez are set for an event in Bakersfield decrying President Donald Trump's policies and economic disparities.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries: SCOTUS should hold Trump admin in contempt over deported Salvadoran migrant
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries is demanding that the Supreme Court sholds the Trump administration in contempt over its deportation of Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, an El Salvadorian illegal migrant and former Maryland resident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin defies court over Maryland deportation, ignites legal showdown
item.description: Trump officials face growing legal pressure after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of a wrongfully deported Maryland man or assist in his return from El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Core Democratic groups are preparing to be targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As President Donald Trump pushes the historical boundaries of executive power, some of the Democratic Party's core political institutions are preparing for the possibility that the federal government may soon launch criminal investigations against them.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: West Point graduate becomes the first woman to compete in the grueling Army Ranger contest
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A female Army Ranger for the first time competed in the annual Best Ranger Competition, and her two-soldier team finished the grueling three-day event over the weekend and came in 14th overall. First Lt. Gabrielle White, and her teammate, Capt.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin's DOT announces deal for high-tech border checkpoint in crucial California sector
item.description: The Trump administration is set to announce a new state-of-the-art border facility near the existing Otay Mesa crossing in the San Diego-Baja, California, region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge greenlights DOJ's motion to drop gun case against Salvadoran, accused MS-13 leader
item.description: A Virginia judge granted the Justice Department's motion to dismiss its suit against accused MS-13 gang member, Henrry Josue Villatoro Santos, who was arrested in late March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens Harvard's tax exempt status
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of revoking Harvard University's tax exempt status, an escalation of the funding fight between the federal government and the Ivy League school.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia prosecutor seeks a third term, saying progressivism is ascendant despite Trump setbacks
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The Philadelphia prosecutor who became a campaign trail punching bag for Republicans amid rising crime in U.S. cities during the coronavirus pandemic is running for reelection on the back end of a withering effort to oust progressive district attorneys nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois Democrats mull next moves after strong showing in April elections
item.description: Gov. JB Pritzker and top state Democrats gathered Monday at a union hall in DuPage County to celebrate the party's near clean sweep of Illinois municipal elections two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Trump sued over 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: President Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs came under their first major legal challenge Monday, brought by a libertarian public-interest firm that argues the president overstepped his authority.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What to know as Social Security anti-fraud measures take effect
item.description: Individuals who are flagged will be required to prove their identity in person for their Social Security claim to be processed.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: A retrial of Sarah Palin's case against 'New York Times' begins Monday
item.description: Former Alaska Gov. Sarah Palin has the rare opportunity to retry her defamation case against The New York Times even though she lost it — twice in a 24-hour-period — in early 2022.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Trump sits cageside at Miami UFC event in his latest appearance at a sports event
item.description: President Donald Trump said the standing ovation and cheers he drew when he took his seat at a UFC event in his home state of Florida were signs “we're doing a good job.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida education bill sparks debate over funds for advanced placement programs
item.description: Florida lawmakers battled it out on the House floor this week over a bill that could lead to significant funding cuts to education.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: GOP ethics complaint alleges conflict by DFL Senate President Bobby Joe Champion
item.description: Six GOP lawmakers said the Minneapolis Democrat broke Senate rules when he carried legislation funding the nonprofit of a former legal client.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York bill aims to help homeless people and their pets stay together
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new state bill aims to expand access to co-sheltering, allowing homeless people and domestic abuse survivors to stay together with their pets during a housing crisis. Senate Bill S673, sponsored by New York state Sen. Monica R.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11
